# Extracao: blogpost-4

OK, I have had a TON of questions coming in...
both in comments on this blog and in my email. So
without further ado, I am going to answer the ones
that keep popping up:

Question: What does it cost?

Answer: OK, I am going to give a bit of a
copywriting lesson here.  :-)

Any time you make any type of sale, one of the key
considerations is how you "reveal price". In other
words, how do you go about telling people how much
your "widget" is going to cost?

This is a very key thing... and how you do it will
make a big impact on your results. 

The most important principle is that you can NEVER
reveal the price until you have properly built the
value of the offer. When you drive by a Mercedes
dealer, you don't see big red signs in their
window that says "Only $79,995".

No, you have to walk in the door and talk to a
sales person and probably take a test ride before
you can get down to brass tacks and find out what
the car will really cost.

When you walk in a jewelry store to buy a Rolex,
you won't see the price tags. First you are going
to talk to a sales rep and get some type of a
pitch as you learn what the price is.

Unless we are talking about a low-end Walmart type
sale, there has to be some education or
explanation that builds the value of the product
for the prospect... and that has to happen BEFORE
you reveal the price.

Think of this like a great big, old-fashioned
scale... the kind of scale that you weigh things.
The one where you balance things on the two sides
of the scale.

So on that scale... on one side you have all the
benefits of the product. On the other side is the
price of the product. Basically, what you want to
do is create a giant huge pile of benefits on the
one side before you ever talk about the price on
the other side.

So that's a long way of telling you this... as
soon as we can tell you every piece of what's
included in the course, then we will tell you what
the price of this Copy That Sells course. 

That will happen on October 18th at 12 noon
eastern time.

What can I say... a tele-seminar with the master

5. A 90-minute 'You-Get-To-See-It-All' tele-

Question: I currently own XYZ course, how is yours
different?

Answer: Obviously, it wouldn't be wise for us to
comment on other courses or other copywriters. So
I will just say this...

Not too long ago I was a complete newbie
copywriter... so I can vividly remember what it
was like just starting out.

I went from newbie to 19 different million dollar
letters in very short order. 

I did that because I studied hard... and
specifically because I studied Dan Kennedy's
material. 

In fact, I didn't just study it, I completely
immersed myself in it - I absorbed it like a
sponge. And, I have to mention, I also had
personal access to Dan during some of this
time.

In this new Copy That Sells course, I have
distilled everything I learned - in my studies AND
through my experience of writing winning copy
nearly every day.

I keep telling you about those 19 sales letters
that were home-runs. Well, there were a lot of
others that weren't home-runs, especially when I
was starting out. There were lots of lessons
learned.

This course takes ALL those lessons and it turns
them into my copywriting formula. And not only
that, it gives them to you step by step by step.

This isn't a bunch of CDs or DVDs that are going
to end up in a pile on your floor. It is an online
learning system that will walk you through the
material in sequential order.

Of course, if you want to skip ahead or bounce
around in the course, they you are welcome to...
and the system will accommodate you.

But it's designed from the ground up to walk you
through every step I use in when I sit down to
write copy. It has become very formulaic for me -
and this course goes through every step of that
formula.
